issues.body: `serializer_versions_pinned` collects every workspace package named oxrdf/oxttl and then keeps only versions starting with `0.3.`/`0.2.`. It never proves that those are the versions resolved for sparq-canon. For example, if sparq-canon moves to oxrdf 0.4 while another workspace crate still resolves oxrdf 0.3.3, `oxrdf03` remains exactly `["0.3.3"]` and this test passes, contrary to the documented guarantee that a serializer bump fails loudly. The same false-negative applies to oxttl. This undermines the load-bearing purpose of the tracking suite.
issues.fix_hint: Resolve the dependency versions specifically for the sparq-canon package and relevant feature, rather than filtering all lockfile entries by the expected version prefix. A small test using Cargo metadata/package dependency edges, or a checked source that unambiguously binds sparq-canon's direct oxrdf/oxttl dependency to the resolved package IDs, should assert the complete actual versions without first discarding unexpected major/minor versions.
issues.title: Version pin can pass after sparq-canon moves to an unreviewed serializer version

verdict.summary: The byte-exact cases are useful and non-vacuous, but the version-pin test does not actually identify the oxrdf/oxttl versions used by sparq-canon. It filters the global lockfile by the old major/minor lines, so a dependency migration can evade the intended re-verification gate when another workspace dependency retains the pinned line.
